title: gentiana verna subsp. arctica
intro: Gentiana verna subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic gentian, is a plant species of the gentian family that is native to the Arctic tundra regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of gentiopicroside and other bioactive compounds. Consuming the plant or its extracts has been associated with various health benefits, including antioxidant, immunomodulatory, and gastrointestinal protective effects. In traditional herbal medicine, gentian root preparations are often used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, and to enhance the body's natural defenses.
